ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUN 18, 2026 · BUILT JUN 17 ~18:25 ET

ES Trading Plan — Jun 18 ETH & RTH

Charts: TPO profile + 30-min RTH/ETH + Daily + VX1! (18:22–18:25 ET) · Catalyst: Hawkish FOMC — Chair Warsh signals rates "may need to move higher," dot-plot splits, markets price hikes by Q1 2027 · MOC: S&P −$2,505.8M SELL imbalance
Last 7,528.50–7,529.00 (+35.75)
Jun 17 RTH: O 7,595.50 · H 7,605.75 · L 7,472.00 · C 7,492.00
POC 7,582.00 · VAH 7,600.75 · VAL 7,540.50
Spike base (new) 7,497.00 · single prints 7,513.50 / 7,505.25
vs 20-EMA 7,518.25 +10.25
vs 50-EMA 7,390.25 +138.25 · ATR 106.75
VIX 18.95 ▼ (−1.58%)
▼ What changed today (Jun 17) — and why the map flips defensive Price opened at 7,595.50 (inside value) and held the upper distribution all morning, but the 2:00 PM FOMC turned the tape: the Fed held rates but new projections showed a sharply split committee (nine officials see at least one hike this year, six of those two-plus; nine see flat-to-lower), and new Chair Kevin Warsh leaned hard on "price stability," declining to give his own dot. Markets moved to fully price a hike by October. ES sold off from VAH 7,600.75 through POC 7,582, VAL 7,540.50, and air-pocketed to TODAY LOW 7,472 on a 2-year yield surge (+16 bp to 4.21%). The late tape carved a spike base at 7,497, two single prints at 7,513.50 / 7,505.25, and an RTH close at 7,492 — but ETH has already V-bounced back to ~7,528.50, reclaiming the single prints and parking just under the daily 20-EMA 7,518.25. MOC printed a −$2,505.8M S&P sell imbalance (NDX −$2,372.8M, Dow −$554.9M, Mag7 −$686.9M) — heavy institutional de-risking into the bell. VIX still fell to 18.95, a notable risk-on/risk-off split. New annotations: 30-min red fib extend 7,621.75 and 7,565.00; lower fib extend 7,396.50; profile labels spike base 7,497, single prints 7,513.50/7,505.25, VAH/POC/VAL band.

01Top-Down Analysis

Reference: last 7,528.50–7,529.00, sitting on the new single-print band and the daily 20-EMA, ~37 pts above the RTH close — the overnight has already repaired most of the FOMC flush.

a) Daily — MA stack & drawn levels

MAValueDistanceStatus
20-EMA7,518.25+10.25PINNED ON IT — flattening/rolling over; the day's pivot — close above = bull intact, close below = momentum lost
50-EMA7,390.25+138.25RECLAIMED — rising; first major dynamic support far below
100-EMA7,233.00+295.50Rising — no near-term magnet
200-EMA7,033.50+495.00Rising
300-EMA6,868.00+660.50Rising
Drawn level (Daily)ColorPriceDistanceNote
Range High (label)Label7,694.75+166.25Recent swing high / ATH-area reference
ATRBox106.75—Full-day budget; today's range 134 = 1.25×
Range Low (label)Label6,415.75−1,112Macro reference (Apr flush low)

Daily narrative: Today printed a wide-range bearish candle (close 7,492 from a 7,605.75 high) on volume 14.87K — a clear FOMC distribution day that engulfed the prior two sessions, but the wick into 7,472 was bought and ETH has clawed back to 7,528.50. Structurally the daily is at an inflection: price closed the RTH session below the 20-EMA but the overnight has reclaimed it. The full MA stack (50/100/200/300) is still rising well below, so the larger uptrend is intact — this is a momentum-loss / value-correction, not a trend break. The tell is whether price accepts above 7,518.25 (20-EMA) or rolls back under it toward the 50-EMA shelf. The whole post-flush rally left no value between 7,540 and 7,580 — a void that cuts both ways.

b) 30-Min RTH+ETH — session flow & drawn levels

30-min narrative: The week ran balance (7,448–7,508) → trend up to a poor high 7,648.75 → balance under the highs → today's FOMC liquidation. The 2 PM leg was one-time-frame down through every shelf (7,578 / 7,565 / 7,550 / VAL 7,540) with no pause until 7,472, leaving thin/single-print structure 7,505–7,513. ETH then V-recovered straight back into the green zone 7,508–7,527 and is pinned under the 20-EMA 7,518.25 with the 7,527.50 line capping. Above is a void back to VAL 7,540.50 then the fib 7,565; below, the spike base 7,497 is the line in the sand before the 7,472 low and the deeper 7,448.50 green zone.

c) Market Profile — Jun 17 session

MetricValueRead
OPEN / CLOSE7,595.50 / 7,492.00Opened inside value → closed −103.5 on the FOMC flush (spike close)
TODAY HIGH / LOW7,605.75 / 7,472.00133.75-pt range (1.25× ATR)
VAH / POC / VAL7,600.75 / 7,582.00 / 7,540.50Value still anchored to the pre-FOMC upper distribution — close printed 48.5 BELOW VAL (downside spike unbacked by value)
Spike base (labeled)7,497.00Late-day capitulation base; Dalton spike rules govern tomorrow's open (inverted — bear spike)
Single prints (labeled)7,513.50 and 7,505.25Two one-TPO ladders from the flush — speed zone now being repaired from below by ETH
ShapeP-base + vertical down-leg (double distribution)Fat upper balance 7,578–7,605, thin elevator down to 7,472
STRUCTURE MAP (Jun 17 close → ETH 7,528.50) 7,582.00 ── POC ◄ value magnet above the void 7,565.00 ── fib extend (red) 7,550.50 ── pivot 7,540.50 ── VAL ◄ first big test above 7,529.00 ── 30m last / 7,527.50 line 7,528.50 ◄◄ YOU ARE HERE (ETH) 7,518.25 ── daily 20-EMA ◄ the pivot 7,513.50 ── SINGLE PRINT ░ speed ░ 7,505.25 ── SINGLE PRINT ░ speed ░ 7,497.00 ── SPIKE BASE ◄ tonight's line in the sand 7,492.00 ── RTH CLOSE 7,491.50 ── 30m pivot 7,472.00 ── TODAY LOW 7,469.50 ── pivot / green zone edge 7,448.50 ── green zone (7,440–7,455) 7,421.25 ── pivot 7,396.50–7,410 ── fib extend / lower green zone

Profile narrative: Classic FOMC distribution: a P-shaped upper balance held all morning (POC 7,582, VAL 7,540.50) until Warsh's "price stability" framing triggered a vertical leg that built no value below VAL. The 7,492 settlement sits 48.5 pts under VAL with two single-print ladders (7,513.50 / 7,505.25) and a spike base at 7,497. The inversion vs the template: this is a bear spike close, so spike rules run the other way — if ETH/RTH holds above 7,497–7,513, the market is repairing the flush (mean-revert up toward VAL 7,540 / POC 7,582 = the void fill); if it loses 7,497, the spike is being accepted lower and 7,472 / 7,448.50 come into play. ETH has already reclaimed both single prints — early repair signal — but the −$2.5B sell MOC says institutions exited size, so chase nothing into the void.

d) VIX + macro context

VX1! 18.95 (−1.58%), low 18.20 — notably, VIX FELL on an equity-down day, an unusual risk-off/vol-down split that argues the selloff was a positioning/rate-repricing event (orderly de-risk) rather than a fear spike. Vol has been grinding lower from the April 31+ highs all quarter; sub-19 keeps the larger melt regime technically alive. Macro: FOMC held rates (4th straight hold) but the SEP dots split sharply — nine officials see ≥1 hike in 2026, six of those two-plus; nine see flat/lower; new Chair Kevin Warsh gave no dot and stressed "price stability," dismissing any move off the 2% target. Markets now fully price a hike by October and two hikes by Q1 2027; 2-yr yields +16 bp to 4.21%, dollar firmer. MOC: S&P −$2,505.8M, NDX −$2,372.8M, Dow −$554.9M, Mag7 −$686.9M sell imbalances — broad institutional de-risk. The asymmetry: a hawkish hold is now priced, so the marginal surprise risk shifts to dovish Fedspeak / soft data (squeeze fuel) — but any hot inflation print or hawkish follow-through re-arms the downside.

⚑ Controlling clusters for Jun 18 7,505.25–7,518.25 (20-EMA + single prints — where price sits now, the immediate decision) · 7,492–7,497 (spike base + close — the polarity line; lose it and the flush re-arms) · 7,540.50–7,550.50 (VAL + pivot — first big upside test / void edge) · 7,578–7,582 (POC — the void-fill magnet) · 7,469.50–7,472 (today's low — must-hold for the repair thesis).

03Context Summary — Jun 17 session

DimensionReadingImplication
Day typeNews-driven trend day DOWN (P-base + vertical leg + spike close)Inverted spike rules govern the open; flushes are typically followed by acceptance-or-repair, rarely immediate re-trend
Settlement location7,492 RTH close / ETH 7,528.50 — 48.5 BELOW VAL but back at the 20-EMAWeak settle, strong recovery — the overnight is voting repair; value placement Thursday is the tell
Profile shapeDouble distribution: fat upper balance 7,578–7,605, thin down-leg, single prints 7,513.50 & 7,505.25, spike 7,497Void above (7,513→7,540) fills fast in either direction
Value migrationPOC 7,582, but close −90 below POCValue far ABOVE price — repair pulls toward 7,540/7,582; rejection there confirms distribution
Price vs POC−53.5 below POC (ETH)Below value — mean-reversion UP is the base path unless a fresh hawkish catalyst

ATR / rangeATR 106.75; today's range 134 (1.25×)Expanded regime; size discipline on

FlowsMOC −$2,505.8M SPX sell; 2yr +16bp to 4.21%Heavy institutional de-risk into the bell — overhead supply is real; respect rejections at VAL/POC

04What-If Scenarios — ETH (Jun 17 → Jun 18)

N1 · Void-Fill Repair — Grind to VAL 7,540 / POC 7,582BULL repair★★★★ 30%
Zone / contextETH holds the single-print band (7,505–7,518) and one-time-frames up through 7,527.50; current trajectory.
NarrativeThe thin down-leg fills fast — VAL 7,540.50 first, then the fib 7,565 and the POC 7,582 magnet. This is mean-reversion into value, not a new uptrend; expect supply at VAL/POC.
SetupLong 7,514–7,520 on holds of the single-print band / 20-EMA · Stop 7,505 (under lower single print) · T1 7,540.50 (+22) · T2 7,565 (+47) · T3 7,582 (+66) · R:R 1.5→4.7
Confirm / invalidConfirm: 30-min closes hold ≥ 7,513.50. Invalid: close < 7,497 → N3.
N2 · 20-EMA Rejection — Range Inside the VoidNEUTRAL fade★★★ 22%
Zone / contextPrice stalls under 7,527.50–7,529, fails to take VAL 7,540.50; chops 7,497–7,529.
NarrativeOvernight balances the spike: the −$2.5B sell MOC weighs, VAL caps, and the single-print band acts as a magnet. Two-sided rotation until RTH gives direction.
SetupFade edges: short 7,527–7,531 stop 7,538 → T 7,510/7,498; long 7,499–7,505 stop 7,491 → T 7,518/7,527 · R:R ~1.6→3.0 per rotation
Confirm / invalidConfirm: two failed pushes through 7,529. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,497–7,540.
N3 · Spike Acceptance Lower — Lose 7,497, Retest 7,472BEAR continuation★★★ 20%
Zone / contextETH loses the spike base 7,497 and the 7,492 close; the V-bounce fails.
NarrativeFresh hawkish Fedspeak or Asia de-risk pushes price back to the 7,472 low. A clean hold there = double-bottom long; a break = the deeper 7,448.50 green zone opens.
SetupShort 7,495 on loss of 7,497 · Stop 7,506 · T1 7,492 (+3) · T2 7,472 (+23) then assess · R:R scalp — PREFER to USE the retest as a long at 7,472–7,475, stop 7,464, T 7,497/7,513/7,527 (R:R up to 4.7)
Confirm / invalidConfirm: no bid on the 7,500 retest. Invalid for the dip-long: 30-min close < 7,469.50 → N4.
N4 · Deep Repair — 7,448.50 Green-Zone BuyBEAR rotation★★ 13%
Zone / context7,472 fails; the whole flush extends toward the next demand shelf.
NarrativePrice air-pockets to the 7,440–7,455 green zone (7,448.50) — the prior week's balance base and a major responsive-buy location ahead of Thursday RTH.
SetupResponsive long 7,445–7,452 at the green zone · Stop 7,436 · T1 7,472 (+24) · T2 7,492 (+44) · T3 7,513 (+65) · R:R 2.4→6.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX still ≤ 20 (orderly). Invalid: 30-min close < 7,440 → N5 regime.
N5 · Hawkish Follow-Through — Toward 7,421 / Fib 7,396.50BEAR tail★ 8%
Zone / contextOvernight hawkish headline (Fedspeak, hot Asian/EU print, yields breaking higher) breaks the 7,440 green zone.
NarrativeRisk-off extends: 7,421.25 pivot → lower green zone / fib 7,396.50 → 50-EMA 7,390.25. The full week's gains compress toward the rising 50-EMA.
SetupNo fresh shorts into the hole. Pre-stage responsive longs 7,398–7,408 (fib + 50-EMA confluence) · Stop 7,388 · T1 7,421 · T2 7,448 · R:R 2.0→4.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: yields ripping, VIX > 21. Invalid: 50-EMA fails → 7,360/7,340, stand down.
N6 · Dovish Squeeze — Full Void Fill > POC, Reclaim 7,600BULL outlier★ 7%
Zone / contextDovish overnight headline (Fed source walk-back, soft data); VIX breaks under 18.
NarrativeThe whole FOMC flush is unwound: one-time-frame up through VAL 7,540 → POC 7,582 → VAH 7,600.75; today's distribution rejected entirely.
SetupDon't chase; buy the first 30-min pullback holding 7,540 from above · Stop 7,530 · T1 7,565 · T2 7,582 · T3 7,600.75 · R:R 1.4→4.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: volume expansion on the headline. Invalid: back under 7,527.50.

05Probability Matrix — ETH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1N1 Void-fill repair → VAL 7,540 / POC 7,582Bull-repair30%★★★★
2N2 20-EMA rejection → range 7,497–7,529Neutral22%★★★
3N3 Lose 7,497 → 7,472 retestBear-shallow20%★★★
4N4 Deep repair → 7,448.50 buyBear-rotation13%★★
5N5 Hawkish follow-through → 7,396/50-EMABear tail8%★
6N6 Dovish squeeze → reclaim 7,600Bull outlier7%★
CombinedBull/repair 37%Bear 41%Neutral 22%
Base case (ETH)Repair-then-stall: ETH holds the single-print band (7,505–7,518) and fills the void up toward VAL 7,540.50, where the −$2.5B sell MOC and overhead supply cap the first attempt, setting up a two-sided RTH decision vs the 20-EMA 7,518.25 and the 7,492–7,497 spike base. Reasoning: price closed below value with value sitting above (mean-reversion pulls up), VIX fell (orderly), and ETH already reclaimed both single prints. The counterweight — heavy institutional sell MOC and a freshly hawkish Fed — argues this is a repair, not a trend resumption; fade the first VAL/POC tag rather than chasing the void.

06Decision Map — ETH

ETH @ 7,528.50 (spike base 7,497 below · 20-EMA 7,518.25 · VAL 7,540.50 above)
│
├─► HOLDS ≥ 7,505 single-print band (repair — current state)
│     ├─ pushes > 7,529 → fills void to VAL 7,540.50
│     │      ├─ rejected at VAL/7,565 → N2 range fade back to 7,513/7,497
│     │      └─ accepts > 7,550 → N1/N6 → POC 7,582 → VAH 7,600
│     └─ stalls under 7,529 → N2 range 7,497–7,529
│
└─► LOSES 7,505 → tests spike base 7,497 / close 7,492
      ├─ holds 7,492–97 → repair intact, rotation only
      └─ closes < 7,497 → N3 → retest TODAY LOW 7,472

            └─ 7,469.50 fails → N4 → green zone 7,448.50

Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 106.75, post-FOMC tape, heavy sell MOC: 50–60% size on void-fill momentum, 70% on the 7,472 / 7,448.50 responsive buys. No shorts as primary trades — the only short is the N3 scalp (loss of 7,497), and that fill is better used as a long at 7,472. Hard rule: a fresh hawkish/yields-up headline = flat first, read later. Respect VAL/POC rejections — the void cuts both ways.

09Structural Warnings

⚠ TWO single-print ladders: 7,513.50 and 7,505.25Today's flush built no value between 7,513 and 7,540. Both ladders are speed zones — moves through them (up or down) will be fast. Don't place passive stops mid-ladder; structure risk at 7,505 / 7,497 / 7,472 instead.
⚠ Spike base 7,497 — inverted Dalton open rules tomorrowThis is a BEAR spike close, so the rules run the other way. Open BELOW 7,492 = spike accepted lower (sell rallies, 7,472 target). Open WITHIN 7,492–7,513 = balancing the spike (two-sided). Open ABOVE 7,513.50 = spike REJECTED (repair confirmed — buy dips toward VAL 7,540 / POC 7,582). This single reference sets Thursday's template.
⚠ Structural void above: 7,513 → 7,540 → 7,582The rally built no value here on the way down. Any RTH revisit moves fast and rejections at VAL 7,540.50 / fib 7,565 / POC 7,582 are high-probability on first tag — take profits there, don't assume continuation.
⚠ −$2.5B sell MOC = real overhead supplyInstitutions de-risked size into the close (SPX −$2,505.8M, Mag7 −$686.9M). The repair has to fight that inventory — VAL and POC are where it shows up. This is the strongest argument against chasing the void-fill.
⚠ Hawkish Fed = priced; the new surprise risk is two-sidedA hike by October is now fully discounted, so a dovish walk-back or soft data is squeeze fuel (N6), while a hot inflation print or hawkish Fedspeak re-arms the downside (N5). Headline risk cuts both ways into Thursday.
ℹ VIX 18.95 — regime gateVol FELL on a down day (orderly repricing, not panic). Sub-19 keeps the melt regime technically alive and supports the repair read; a break back above 21 would flip the template bearish regardless of price.

10Session Transition — ETH → Thursday RTH open

If price at zone into RTH openImplicationLikely day type
> 7,582 (above POC)Full void filled overnight; flush rejectedReversal/trend-up; buy dips to VAL 7,540
7,540–7,582Repair into value — decision at POCNormal-variation up; fade POC 7,582 first tag
7,513.50–7,540 (in void)Spike rejected, repairing toward VALTwo-sided; trade the 7,540 VAL / 7,518 20-EMA polarity
7,492–7,513.50 (in spike)Balancing the spike base — two-sidedRotational; trade the 7,497 polarity, watch for direction off the open
7,472–7,492Spike accepted lower; close zone testedRepair day from 7,472; double-bottom long if it holds
< 7,472Both ladders + low broken — hawkish follow-throughTrend-down to 7,448.50 → 7,421 → fib 7,396.50

11Final Summary — ETH

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL (intact, weakened) — full MA stack rising below price; FOMC is a momentum-loss event, not a trend break
Intermediate biasNEUTRAL / distributing — poor high 7,648, FOMC distribution day, value far above price; needs to reclaim VAL/POC to repair
Overnight biasREPAIR-UP into supply — buy structure, fade VAL/POC, never chase the void
Base caseRepair-then-stall: hold 7,505–7,518, fill void to VAL 7,540.50, first tag rejected; two-sided into RTH
Primary tradeLong 7,514–7,520 (single-print/20-EMA), stop 7,505, targets 7,540.50 / 7,565 / 7,582
Best-location alternateLong 7,472–7,475 on a 7,497 loss + low retest, stop 7,464 → 7,492 / 7,513 / 7,527
Key bull level7,540.50 VAL → 7,582 POC (acceptance = void filled, flush rejected, 7,600 unlocks)
Key bear level7,497 spike base (loss re-opens 7,472 → 7,448.50 → fib 7,396.50)
Biggest riskHeavy sell MOC + hawkish Fed: void-fill stalls and reverses at VAL/POC into a deeper flush; two-sided headline risk overnight
Night-type expectationRepair to VAL/POC (≈30%) · range in the void 7,497–7,529 (≈22%) · re-test 7,472 (≈20%) · deeper flush (≈21%) · dovish squeeze (≈7%)

01Top-Down — Thursday Jun 18 RTH framing

Same chart evidence as the ETH tab (full tables there). Re-cast for the day session. Verify overnight extremes at the bell and shift the inverted-spike-rule template accordingly.

Daily: Yesterday's FOMC distribution candle closed below the 20-EMA but ETH reclaimed it. Price is pinned on 20-EMA 7,518.25 (+10.25 from last) — the day's binary. A close back above it Thursday keeps the V-repair alive and re-targets VAL 7,540 → POC 7,582 → VAH 7,600.75; a rejection there rolls toward the rising 50-EMA 7,390.25. ATR 106.75; the full MA stack below is still rising (uptrend intact).

30-min: One-time-frame DOWN from 7,605.75 with a thin void 7,513→7,540 and single prints 7,513.50/7,505.25. Working ladder below: spike base 7,497, close 7,492, low 7,472, green zone 7,448.50, pivot 7,421.25, fib 7,396.50. Above: 7,527.50 line, VAL 7,540.50, pivot 7,550.50, fib 7,565, POC 7,582, VAH 7,600.75, high 7,605.75, poor high 7,648.75.

Profile: Jun 17 = P-base + news down-leg + spike close (inverted). Thursday's tell is where value builds: value rebuilding back up into 7,540–7,582 ratifies the repair; value holding/building below 7,505 says the flush is being accepted and 7,472/7,448 come next. Inverted Dalton spike rules vs 7,497 set the opening template (see Warnings).

VIX/macro: VX1 18.95 and falling = the selloff was orderly rate-repricing, supportive of repair. Thursday-specific: hawkish hike-by-October is priced, so the marginal surprise skews dovish (squeeze) — but watch the 2-yr yield (4.21% +16bp) and any Fedspeak; a fresh hawkish push re-arms the downside. Yesterday's MOC: −$2,505.8M SPX SELL — respect overhead supply at VAL/POC.

02Key Levels — RTH working ladder

Master table on the ETH tab. RTH-critical subset (ref 7,528.50; re-anchor to ON extremes pre-open):
ZonePriceWhy it matters Thursday★
R57,648.75 / 7,632.25Poor high / swing — stretch target only on a dovish reversal★★★
R4 🔗7,600.00–7,605.75VAH + yesterday's high — full-reversal ceiling★★★★
R3 🔗7,578.25–7,582.00POC — the void-fill magnet, prime fade-the-first-tag zone★★★★★
R27,565.00Fib extend (red) — mid-void resistance★★★★
R1 🔗7,540.50–7,550.50VAL + pivot — first big overhead test / void edge★★★★★
PIVOT7,505.25–7,518.2520-EMA + single prints + 7,527.50 line — the decision band★★★★★
S17,492.00–7,497.00Spike base + close — polarity; lose it and the flush re-arms★★★★★
S2 🔗7,469.50–7,472.00TODAY LOW + pivot — must-hold for repair★★★★★
S37,440.00–7,455.00Green zone (7,448.50) — deep responsive buy★★★★
S47,421.25 / 7,396.50Pivot / fib extend — hawkish-extension targets★★★
S57,390.2550-EMA — bull thesis defended here★★★★

03Context Into the Thursday Open

DimensionReadingImplication
Prior day typeNews trend-day DOWN with spike close (FOMC)Inverted spike rules govern the open; flush days are typically followed by acceptance-or-repair, rarely immediate re-trend lower
Open vs spike<7,492 accept-lower · 7,492–7,513 balance · >7,513.50 reject (repair)The first 30 minutes vs 7,497 sets the template
Value placementYesterday's value 7,540–7,600 — far ABOVE priceThursday repair must BUILD value back up to 7,540+ to ratify; watch the developing VA from the open
InventoryShorts from 7,540–7,580 in profit; ON V-buyers long from 7,472–7,505Short-covering pop into VAL is normal and fadeable; ON longs defend 7,505
Day-of-week / eventThursday, post-FOMC digestionOften a fade/repair day after the event leg; watch yields & Fedspeak for the swing factor
ATR budget106.75 — full-repair day ≈ 7,580–7,600; full-bear ≈ 7,420–7,450Both extremes map onto POC/VAH and the green zone

04What-If Scenarios — RTH Thursday Jun 18

F1 · Repair-Accepted Open → Void Fill → VAL/POC FadeBULL-repair base case★★★★ 30%
Open / contextOpens 7,513–7,540 (above the spike, in the void). Early dip to 7,505–7,514 bought, then the run at VAL 7,540.50.
NarrativeValue rebuilds 7,510–7,545; the void fills to VAL then the fib 7,565 / POC 7,582, where the sell-MOC supply caps the first attempt. Fade the first POC tag.
SetupLong 7,505–7,514 (single-print/20-EMA zone) · Stop 7,496 · T1 7,540.50 (+30) · T2 7,565 (+55) · T3 7,582 (+72) · R:R 1.8→4.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: first 30-min holds > 7,505. Invalid: 30-min close below 7,497 → F3.
F2 · Dovish Gap-and-Go → Reclaim POC/VAHBULL reversal★★★ 15%
Open / contextOpens > 7,540 on a dovish overnight headline / soft data; VIX < 18.
NarrativeVAL reclaimed at the bell; one-time-frame up through 7,565 to POC 7,582 → VAH 7,600.75; FOMC distribution fully rejected.
SetupLong first pullback holding 7,540 from above · Stop 7,530 · T1 7,565 · T2 7,582 · T3 7,600.75 · R:R 1.5→5.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: gap holds first 30 min. Invalid: re-entry below 7,527.50 = void-fade risk back to 7,505.
F3 · Spike Accepted Lower → 7,472 Retest → Responsive BuyREPAIR then BULL★★★ 22%
Open / contextOpens/breaks below 7,497 — spike accepted; selling continues into the morning.
Narrative7,492 close zone fails, the 7,472 low retests; buyers respond there (double-bottom) or at the 7,448.50 green zone; afternoon recovery toward the spike base.
Setup(a) Scalp short 7,494 → 7,475/7,472 (tight, stop 7,503). (b) Core trade: long 7,472–7,475 or 7,445–7,452 on responsive turn · Stop 7,464/7,436 · T1 7,497 · T2 7,513 · R:R up to 5.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: orderly retest, VIX ≤ 20. Invalid: close < 7,440 → F5.
F4 · VAL Rejection → Range Day in the VoidNEUTRAL fade★★ 18%
Open / contextMorning tags 7,540–7,550, rejects; but 7,497–7,505 holds every test below.
NarrativeThursday bracket 7,497–7,540: fade both edges; value overlaps inside the void as the market digests the FOMC. Constructive only if the lower edge holds.
SetupShort 7,538–7,545 stop 7,552 → T 7,518/7,505; Long 7,499–7,505 stop 7,491 → T 7,527/7,540 · R:R 1.6→3.5 per rotation
Confirm / invalidConfirm: two failed edge-breaks by midday. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,497–7,540.
F5 · Hawkish Follow-Through → 7,448 / Fib 7,396.50BEAR tail★★ 10%
Open / contextHawkish Fedspeak / yields breaking higher; 7,472 low fails.
NarrativeContinuation of the FOMC leg: 7,448.50 green zone → 7,421.25 → fib 7,396.50 / 50-EMA 7,390.25, worst case. De-risking amplifies.
SetupShort 7,469 on loss of 7,472 · Stop 7,479 · T1 7,448 (+21) · T2 7,421 (+48) · T3 7,398 (+71) · R:R 2.0→7.0. Then responsive long 7,398–7,408 (fib + 50-EMA) stop 7,388 if VIX < 21.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: yields ripping, headline-driven volume. Invalid: reclaim 7,497.
F6 · Post-FOMC Chop-and-Drift (overlay)LATE-DAY pattern★ 5%
Open / contextQuiet digestion day, no fresh catalyst; price oscillates around the 20-EMA / single-print band.
NarrativeClassic day-after-FOMC: low conviction, tight rotations around 7,505–7,529, value building inside the void. Scalps only; size down.
SetupTrade the band edges only: long 7,505 / short 7,529, ~10-pt targets, tight stops · R:R ~1.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX flat sub-19, no expansion. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,497–7,540 → F1/F3.

05Probability Matrix — RTH

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1F1 Repair accepted → void fill → VAL/POC fadeBull-repair30%★★★★
2F3 Spike accepted lower → 7,472 → responsive buyRepair→Bull22%★★★
3F4 VAL rejection → void-range dayNeutral18%★★
4F2 Dovish gap-and-go → reclaim POC/VAHBull15%★★★
5F5 Hawkish follow-through → 7,448/fib 7,396Bear10%★★
6F6 Post-FOMC chop-and-drift (overlay)Late-day5%★
CombinedBull/repair 67%Bear 10%Neutral 23%
Base case (RTH)Repair-accepted open, early dip into 7,505–7,514 bought, void fills toward VAL 7,540.50, and a real fight / first-tag rejection at the POC 7,582 magnet where the sell-MOC supply sits. Reasoning: price closed below value with value above (mean-reversion up), VIX fell (orderly), and ON already reclaimed both single prints. The bear path (F5) needs a fresh hawkish/yields catalyst — price alone reverts up first. But this is a repair into supply, not a trend day: take VAL/POC profits, don't chase.

06Decision Map — RTH

THURSDAY RTH OPEN vs SPIKE (base 7,497 · close 7,492 · low 7,472 · single prints 7,505/7,513)
│
├─► OPEN > 7,513.50 (spike rejected — repair)
│     ├─ holds 7,513 retest → F1/F2 → VAL 7,540 → 7,565 → POC 7,582
│     └─ fails back into spike → balance 7,497–7,540 (F4)
│
├─► OPEN 7,497–7,513.50 (balancing — most likely)
│     ├─ dip 7,499–7,505 BOUGHT → F1 long → 7,527 → VAL 7,540.50
│     │      ├─ accepted > 7,550 → 7,565 → POC 7,582
│     │      └─ rejected ×2 → F4 range fade edges 7,505↔7,540
│     └─ 30-min close < 7,497 → spike accepted lower → F3
│            └─ 7,492 fails → 7,472 retest → BUY double-bottom / 7,448.50
│                   └─ 7,440 fails (headline) → F5 → 7,421 → fib 7,396.50 / 50-EMA 7,390
│
└─► OPEN < 7,492 (spike accepted lower at the bell)
      ├─ look-below-and-fail, reclaims 7,497 → strong long → 7,513/7,527
      └─ acceptance lower → F3 path above; afternoon recovery attempt

WATCH: 2-yr yield (4.21%) & any Fedspeak — hawkish push = F5, dovish walk-back = F2
LevelHoldsBreaks

7,540.50–7,550 VALFirst-tag rejection (range day)POC 7,582 magnet next

07Bias Flip Conditions — RTH

Starting biasFlip toTriggerAction
Repair (default)Bull-repair confirmTwo 30-min closes > 7,540.50 (VAL reclaim)Hold runners 7,565/7,582; trail under 7,527.50
Bull-repairNeutral / cappedRejection at POC 7,582 ×2Fade back to VAL 7,540 / 7,505; take profits
RepairBear-rotation30-min close < 7,497Flatten; bid 7,472 then 7,448.50
Bear-rotationHawkish bear30-min close < 7,469.50 (esp. yields up)F5 short to 7,421/fib 7,396; no longs until 50-EMA 7,390
Any bearStabilizingReclaim 7,497 + VIX < 20Responsive long 7,500–7,506, half size
AnySqueezeDovish headline → accept > 7,540F2: ride to POC 7,582 / VAH 7,600

VAL/POC Fade Short (F1/F4 reversal)
SHORTup to 3.5R▶
Entry

Stop
7,591 — above the 7,591.50 line · risk 9–13 pts
Targets
T1 7,565 (+17)T2 7,540.50 (+42)T3 7,518/7,513 (+64→+69)
Scaling

R:R
1.3R / 3.2R / 3.5R
Kill switch
Two 30-min closes > 7,582 → repair won, exit and flip to F2 long
Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 106.75 post-FOMC with a −$2.5B sell MOC: 55–65% size, ¼-size on the F3 short scalp. Longs are the higher-conviction side (repair into value); the F3 fade-short is a tactical scalp at the POC magnet, NOT a core position — cover into 7,540/7,513. Hard rule: any hawkish Fedspeak / yields-up headline = flat longs first, reassess.

09Structural Warnings — RTH

⚠ Inverted spike rules at the open (vs base 7,497)Open below 7,492 = spike accepted lower (sell rallies, 7,472 target). Open 7,492–7,513.50 = balancing (trade 7,497 polarity). Open above 7,513.50 = spike rejected (repair confirmed — buy dips toward VAL 7,540 / POC 7,582). This sets Thursday's template.
⚠ Structural void: 7,513 → 7,540 → 7,582The flush built no value here. Any RTH revisit moves fast — entries at labeled edges only (7,540 VAL / 7,565 fib / 7,582 POC), never mid-air. First tags reject with high probability.
⚠ −$2.5B sell MOC = overhead supply at VAL/POCInstitutions de-risked size into the close. The repair has to absorb that inventory — expect the heaviest selling exactly at VAL 7,540.50 and POC 7,582. Fade the first tag; only trust acceptance (2× 30-min closes above).
⚠ Hawkish hold is PRICED — surprise risk is two-sidedA hike by October is discounted. A dovish walk-back/soft data is squeeze fuel (F2); a hot inflation print or hawkish Fedspeak re-arms the downside (F5). Watch the 2-yr yield (4.21%) for the swing tell.
⚠ Day-after-FOMC behaviorThe day after a big event leg is often a fade/digest day — directional morning, lower-conviction afternoon. Don't over-trust either edge of the void; take profits at structure.
ℹ VIX gate< 19 = orderly repair regime (favor F1/F3 longs) · 19–21 = two-sided · > 21 = repair template suspended, F5 in play.

10Session Transition — Thursday close → Friday

If Thursday closes at…ImplicationNext-day lean
> 7,582 (above POC)Void filled, FOMC flush rejected, 20-EMA reclaimedBull repair complete: 7,600 → 7,632 → poor-high 7,648 in play
7,540–7,582Repaired into value but capped at POCBuy-dips inside value 7,540–7,582; POC is the pivot
7,513–7,540 (in void)Partial repair — 20-EMA reclaimed, VAL notTwo-sided; 7,513 must hold Friday, VAL 7,540 the target
7,492–7,513 (in spike)Balanced the spike — digestion modeCoiling; direction off Friday's open vs 7,497
7,472–7,492Spike accepted, repair failed into the closeTwo-sided; 7,472 must hold; bear-rotation below
< 7,472FOMC leg extending — hawkish regimeBear continuation: 7,448 → 7,421 → fib 7,396.50 / 50-EMA

11Final Summary — RTH Thursday Jun 18

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL (intact, weakened) — MA stack rising below price; FOMC is a momentum-loss / value-correction, not a trend break
Intermediate biasNEUTRAL / distributing — poor high 7,648, FOMC distribution day; must reclaim VAL/POC to repair, hold 7,472 to avoid trend-down
Intraday biasREPAIR-UP into supply — buy structured dips, fade VAL/POC first tag, never chase the void
Base caseRepair-accepted open → dip to 7,505–7,514 bought → void fills to VAL 7,540 → first POC 7,582 tag rejected
Primary tradeLong 7,505–7,514, stop 7,496, targets 7,540.50 / 7,565 / 7,582 (F1)
Best-location alternateLong 7,472–7,475 on a 7,497 loss + low retest, stop 7,464 → 7,492 / 7,505 / 7,513 (F3)
Key bull level7,540.50 VAL → 7,582 POC — acceptance ends the flush, unlocks 7,600.75 / 7,632
Key bear level7,497 spike base — close below on a hawkish headline re-opens 7,472 → 7,448.50 → fib 7,396.50
Biggest riskHeavy sell MOC + hawkish Fed: repair stalls/reverses at VAL/POC into a deeper flush; two-sided yields/Fedspeak headline risk
Day-type expectationRepair into value / fade at POC (≈30%) · spike-accepted retest then buy (≈22%) · void range (≈18%) · dovish gap-up (≈15%) · hawkish trend-down (≈10%) · chop (≈5%)
ES Plan for Jun 18, 2026 (ETH + RTH) · built Jun 17 ~18:25 ET from TPO + 30-min + Daily + VX1! charts · Last 7,528.50 · Jun 17 RTH: O 7,595.50 / H 7,605.75 / L 7,472 / C 7,492 · Spike base 7,497 · Single prints 7,513.50/7,505.25 · POC 7,582 / VAH 7,600.75 / VAL 7,540.50 · Catalyst: Hawkish FOMC (Warsh, split dots, hike priced by Oct) · MOC −$2,505.8M SPX · VIX 18.95 · Not financial advice. Re-anchor to overnight extremes pre-open.
